mitchell-james.text: James Gordon Mitchell (Democratic) is a candidate for U.S. Representative, TX-17.
As of 2026-07-04, FEC records show James Gordon Mitchell had raised $19,378 and spent $19,018 for this campaign.
No public voting record in our set for James Gordon Mitchell (not currently a member of Congress).

sessions-pete.text: Pete Sessions (Republican) is the current officeholder running for U.S. Representative, TX-17.
As of 2026-07-04, FEC records show Pete Sessions had raised $901,426 and spent $454,306 for this campaign.

On 7-Jan-2025, Pete Sessions voted Yea on H R 29. A Yea vote required federal detention of unauthorized immigrants charged with theft, burglary, larceny, or assaulting a law enforcement officer, and let state attorneys general sue the federal government over certain immigration enforcement decisions.

base.summary: This is the U.S. House race for TX-17. In the most recent election (2024-11-05), Pete Sessions (REP) won by about 32.7 points out of 291042 votes cast. The bullets below summarize each current candidate's public finance and voting-record data where available.
base.caveats: This summary only includes facts found in our sourced dataset (FEC finance filings and U.S. House Clerk roll-call votes). It omits anything we could not verify from those sources. No public data in our set on any candidate's policy positions for this race (the positions field was empty for every candidate as of this build). No public data in our set on James Gordon Mitchell's policy positions. No public data in our set on Pete Sessions's policy positions.
